Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendants Rollie R. French, Inc., And George R. Townsend Associates Inc.
Off Calendar Nov-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Tiffany Viveros, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Jim Harmuth, who was not present at today's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendants Rollie R. French, Inc. or George R. Townsend & Associates, Inc.
Default was entered against defendant Rollie R. French, Inc. on January 10, 2011.
Default was entered against defendant George R. Townsend & Associates, Inc. on March 11, 2010.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on November 13, 2017.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Mario Barbera, and against the Defaulted Defendants Rollie R. French, Inc. and George R. Townsend & Associates, Inc., as follows:

Defendant Rollie R. French, Inc.

Economic Damages - $50,000.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $300,000.00.
Total - $350,000.00.

Defendant George R. Townsend & Associates, Inc.

Economic Damages - $80,000.00.
Non-Economic Damages - $2,000,000.00.
Total - $2,080,000.00.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Harry M. Dorman, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon , CSR#6840.

Oren P. Noah, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, JIM HARMUTH.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that Plaintiff JIM HARMUTH shall recover damages from defendant MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY in the amount of $134,750.00 in Economic Damages, $2,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages, for a total amount of $2,134,750.00
The judgment shall take effect immediately.
The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.
Judgment signed in open court.
